2605.03821.title: RoboAlign-R1: Distilled Multimodal Reward Alignment for Robot Video World Models
2605.03821.paper_summary: Existing robot video world models are typically trained with low-level objectives such as reconstruction and perceptual similarity, which are poorly aligned with the capabilities that matter most for robot decision making, including instruction following, manipulation success, and physical plausibility. They also suffer from error accumulation in long-horizon autoregressive prediction. We present RoboAlign-R1, a framework that combines reward-aligned post-training with stabilized long-horizon inference for robot video world models. We construct RobotWorldBench, a benchmark of 10,000 annotated video-instruction pairs collected from four robot data sources, and train a multimodal teacher judge, RoboAlign-Judge, to provide fine-grained six-dimensional evaluation of generated videos. We then distill the teacher into a lightweight student reward model for efficient reinforcement-learning-based post-training. To reduce long-horizon rollout drift, we further introduce Sliding Window Re-encoding (SWR), a training-free inference strategy that periodically refreshes the generation context. Under our in-domain evaluation protocol, RoboAlign-R1 improves the aggregate six-dimension score by 10.1% over the strongest baseline, including gains of 7.5% on Manipulation Accuracy and 4.6% on Instruction Following; these ranking improvements are further supported by an external VLM-based cross-check and a blinded human study. Meanwhile, SWR improves long-horizon prediction quality with only about 1% additional latency, yielding a 2.8% gain in SSIM and a 9.8% reduction in LPIPS. Together, these results show that reward-aligned post-training and stabilized long-horizon decoding improve task consistency, physical realism, and long-horizon prediction quality in robot video world models.
2605.03821.paper_summary_zh: 現有的機器人視頻世界模型通常是以重建和感知相似度等低層次目標進行訓練，這些目標與對機器人決策最重要的能力（包括遵循指令、操作成功和物理合理性）之間的對齊程度較差。它們在長期自回歸預測中也遭受了錯誤累積。我們提出了RoboAlign-R1，一個結合了獎勵對齊後訓練和穩定長期推理的框架，用於機器人視頻世界模型。我們構建了RobotWorldBench，一個包含從四個機器人數據來源收集的10,000對註釋視頻-指令對的基準，並訓練了一個多模態教師評估者RoboAlign-Judge，以提供生成視頻的細緻六維評估。然後，我們將教師提煉成一個輕量級學生獎勵模型，以便進行高效的基於增強學習的後訓練。為了減少長期展開漂移，我們進一步引入了滑動窗口重新編碼（SWR），這是一種無需訓練的推理策略，定期刷新生成上下文。在我們的域內評估協議下，RoboAlign-R1在最強基線上提高了10.1%的總體六維得分，包括在操作準確性上提高了7.5%和在遵循指令上提高了4.6%；這些排名的改善得到了基於外部VLM的交叉檢查和盲人研究的進一步支持。同時，SWR在僅增加約1%的延遲的情況下改善了長期預測質量，帶來了2.8%的SSIM增益和9.8%的LPIPS減少。總體而言，這些結果表明，獎勵對齊後訓練和穩定的長期解碼改善了機器人視頻世界模型中的任務一致性、物理真實性和長期預測質量。
2605.03821.author: Hao Wu et.al.
2605.03821.authors: Hao Wu, Yuqi Li, Yuan Gao, Fan Xu, Fan Zhang, Kun Wang, Penghao Zhao, Qiufeng Wang, Yizhou Zhao, Weiyan Wang, Yingli Tian, Xian Wu, Xiaomeng Huang
